codeReview.summary: The current button implementation satisfies the requested reusable API and the follow-up fixes cover the earlier loading accessibility and class-merging regressions. I did not find any remaining feature-specific defects in the reviewed files.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `ui-worker` skill still frames repo-wide `npm run lint` as a mandatory fix-all step, but narrow UI component work in this repo can be blocked by unrelated lint noise from `.worktrees` and legacy files. Workers therefore have to deviate even when they have sufficient targeted verification.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/ui-worker/SKILL.md` section `4. 类型检查和代码规范` requires `npm run lint` and says to fix all errors, while the handoff for worker session `9b1960c2-4e85-4d29-91fc-c47c5ee16548` records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=false` because global lint failed in unrelated `.worktrees/...` and pre-existing files outside `src/components/ui/Button.vue` and `src/components/ui/Button.test.ts`.

summary: Pass: reviewed the handoff, transcript skeleton, commit `cae5749`, prior scrutiny context, and the current `Button.vue`/`Button.test.ts`. The feature now meets the button requirements and the earlier follow-up regressions are addressed; only a durable skill-guidance gap was noted.
